headline: Jeff Bezos Says Bottom 50% of Americans Should Pay No Taxes. The Real Culprit Is Federal Spending
summary: During a recent episode of The Pomp Podcast featuring guest Jordi Visser, host Anthony Pompliano highlighted a striking proposal from Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN) founder Jeff Bezos. Bezos believes the bottom 50% of Americans should pay no federal taxes. According to the discussion, the bottom half of earners currently contribute only 3% of the federal budget, which lends credence ... Jeff Bezos Says Bottom 50% of Americans Should Pay No Taxes. The Real Culprit Is Federal Spending

headline: Is the current market backdrop really different from 2022? JPM weighs in
summary: Investing.com -- JPMorgan told investors in a note on Tuesday that it "fundamentally believes that the current backdrop is different to 2022" and that central banks are unlikely to hike as aggressively as markets are pricing, using that view to build a case for adding to low-volatility stocks at current levels.

content.title: Is the current market backdrop really different from 2022? JPM weighs in

headline: How The Braze (BRZE) Investment Story Is Shifting With Mixed AI And Valuation Calls
summary: Braze is back in focus after an analyst fair value estimate shifted slightly to US$34.85 from US$34.95, a small move that still signals a refreshed view of the stock. Behind this tweak is a more mixed research backdrop, where some firms are trimming targets and flagging AI related risks, while others see enough support in the story to inch targets higher. As you read on, you will see how these competing calls shape the evolving narrative you might want to track. Analyst Price Targets don't...

headline: VIG Calls Itself a Dividend Appreciation Fund, But Its 1.5 Percent Yield Reveals What That Really Means
summary: Imagine a retiree who reads that Vanguard Dividend Appreciation ETF (NYSEARCA:VIG) is a top dividend fund, parks $300,000 in it, and waits for the checks to arrive. They get ~$4,500 a year. That is the VIG problem in one sentence. The fund’s 1.5% distribution yield sits right next to the S&P 500’s payout, which means ... headline: EMB’s 6 Percent Emerging Market Bond Yield Hides Hard Currency Sovereign Default Risk Most Income Investors Have Never Modeled
summary: The iShares J.P. Morgan USD Emerging Markets Bond ETF (NASDAQ:EMB) is the default vehicle for retirees and income investors seeking higher coupons than U.S. Treasuries without currency risk. EMB holds roughly $13 billion in assets, charges 0.39%, and pays a distribution yield close to 6% on dollar-denominated sovereign bonds from governments like Mexico, Brazil, Indonesia, ...
